preview_text:
The model rates England as favorite with a 49% win probability, against Croatia's 21%. England's edge reflects the numerical inputs available to the statistical forecast, yet the gap is moderate enough that a Croatia upset remains plausible at better than one-in-five odds.

The draw emerges as a meaningful outcome at 30%, tilting the match toward competitive balance. The spread across outcomes—with England favored but neither team commanding more than half the probability mass—suggests a genuinely contested fixture rather than a heavily one-sided affair. A stalemate or a narrow England victory are both well-represented scenarios.

The sportsbook consensus shifts the England probability to 54%, a gap of roughly 5 percentage points above the model. The books also trim the draw to 26% while Croatia's chance dips slightly to 20%. The divergence is modest; both sources rank England first and converge on Croatia as underdog. The dashboard does not claim to know which probability set proves more accurate.
